title: Formatting Forms to download other than Excel
excerpt: Hello,  Is there a way to download forms into other formats other than excel?  For example, can I download the form submitted as a Word doc?

posts.author: rfrench (Romny French)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, Is there a way to download forms into other formats other than excel? For example, can I download the form submitted as a Word doc?

posts.author: Rua (Acorn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed!
this would be very useful. You can download a PDF from the Form Viewer view but I would like to see this function (and similar view style - individual responses ) right in the form results tab.

posts.author: DelBocaVista (Marty Fishdog)
posts.cookedHtml: Something other than excel would be useful. Anyone know if it’s possible to save the completed form as a pdf using an automation?

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @rfrench , Hi @Rua , Hi @DelBocaVista ,

You can use the app DocExport PDF Generator to create a PDF or Word Document in your own design including information from a form response. If you combine it with the monday automation “When item created”, you can completely automate the process.

After a form submission, a PDF will be created and automatically attached to a file column and/or sent via email to the submitter.

Here’s a quick video tutorial of how to set it up. It’s for the use case ‘Event Registration via Forms’, but it’s a similar use case with the same logic. After form submission, a PDF ticket should be created and placed in a file column:

You can also checkout our free Template Gallery to get some inspiration.
